problem.title: Merge Intervals
problem.description: Given an array of intervals where intervals[i] = [starti, endi], merge all overlapping intervals, and return an array of the non-overlapping intervals that cover all the intervals in the input.
assumptions: Intervals may be given in any order.
Return merged list of non-overlapping intervals.

example.explanation: [1,3] and [2,6] overlap → merge to [1,6]. Sort by start time, then merge overlapping.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Sort and Merge
solutions.description: Sort intervals by start time so that any overlapping intervals are adjacent, then scan once merging each interval into the current one if it overlaps. Two intervals overlap when the current end is at least the next interval's start. The key insight is that sorting guarantees we only need to compare each interval with the one immediately before it.
